mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS signals aggressive monetary easing that lowers discount rates and supports equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle near the zero lower bound
failure_condition: Reject if falling FEDFUNDS observations do not deliver higher 20-day forward returns than non-falling observations, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above a recessionary threshold, expected household income and earnings forecasts deteriorate, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE crossings are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns exceed the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in RSAFS and HOUST identifies a broad private-demand contraction that reduces expected corporate revenue, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: balance-sheet recession with housing and consumption stress
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS-HOUST contraction triggers have non-negative average 60-day forward returns or fail to underperform periods when only one proxy is weak.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in INDPRO signals falling capacity utilization and weaker operating leverage, leading investors to cut near-term earnings expectations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside triggers are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of observations or if triggered returns are not below baseline at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Extremely depressed HOUST combined with near-zero FEDFUNDS marks a policy-supported housing trough setup; reduced financing costs stabilize cyclicals and produce positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 550 and FEDFUNDS was 0.15 in the latest vintage snapshot
regime_hint: post-shock policy support after housing collapse
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-HOUST low-FEDFUNDS state has negative average 60-day forward returns or performs no better than low-HOUST states without low FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Falling CPIAUCSL with already-low FEDFUNDS increases real-income support and confirms policy room, which raises equity valuation multiples and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation plus low-FEDFUNDS observations have average 20-day returns below zero or do not outperform high-inflation low-FEDFUNDS observations.

mechanism_narrative: A negative PAYEMS trend signals accelerating job losses that immediately pressure consumption and earnings guidance, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative PAYEMS momentum is not associated with lower 5-day forward returns than positive PAYEMS momentum, or if the sign hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A GDPC1 contraction crossing a severe recession threshold signals broad real-demand destruction and declining aggregate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth crosses below -2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available vintage date in the snapshot was 2008-10-01
failure_condition: Reject if severe GDPC1 contraction triggers have positive average 60-day forward returns or if they fail to underperform non-contraction quarters by at least 100 basis points.
